sources.full: Valda's Spire of Secrets: Player Pack 2

subclassFeature.name: Pistolero
entries: {@i Fire Six-Shooters with Incredible Speed}

Bullets are power, and you have long believed that more bullets equal more power. Your expertise is in delivering a hail of deadly fire to pulverize your enemies. Not every bullet needs to be accurate to make a difference.

subclassFeature.name: Close-Quarters Shooting
entries: Being within 5 feet of an enemy doesn't impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on your attack rolls with Ranged weapons.

subclassFeature.name: Fan the Hammer [Maneuver]
entries: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action with a Ranged weapon that doesn't have {@itemProperty 2H|XPHB|Two-Handed}, you can expend one Risk Die as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to make two additional ranged attacks with that weapon. These additional attacks always have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}, regardless of circumstance. You can't use the {@itemMastery Automatic|ValdaGunslinger} mastery property with these attacks, and you must have a free hand to use this maneuver.

subclassFeature.name: Disarm
entries: When you score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} and trigger your {@classFeature Gut Shot|Gunslinger|ValdaGunslinger|5} feature against a creature, you can disarm the target instead of lodging a projectile in it. The target drops one object of your choice that it's holding, with the object landing in a space of your choice up to 15 feet away from the target.

subclassFeature.name: Showdown [Maneuver]
entries: When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, you can expend one Risk Die to draw a Ranged weapon and make an attack using it. Add the Risk Die to the damage roll. On a hit, the target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against creatures other than you on the first round of combat.

subclassFeature.name: Bullet Time
entries: Once on each of your turns when you make a ranged attack using a weapon, you can gain {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the roll.

subclassFeature.name: Heroic Sorcery
entries: {@i Wield Swords and Sorcery as a Reincarnated Hero}

You are the reincarnation of a legendary hero known to have slain many terrible foes, and your sorcerous magic draws from your legendary past. Being called back to life, whether by the gods, a very powerful mage, or a cycle of reincarnation, has awakened a well of magic and your ancient instincts for battle.

subclassFeature.name: Heroic Spells
entries: When you reach a Sorcerer level specified in the Heroic Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

entries.caption: Heroic Spells
rows: {@spell Arc Blade|ValdaPlayerPack2}, {@spell Burning Blade|ValdaPlayerPack2}, {@spell Frigid Blade|ValdaPlayerPack2}, {@spell Heroism|XPHB}, {@spell Magic Weapon|XPHB}, {@spell Mirror Image|XPHB}, {@spell Shield|XPHB}

subclassFeature.name: Martial Sorcery
entries: Your past heroic life grants you the following benefits.

entries.name: Heroic Soul
entries: At the start of each of your turns, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to gain {@dice 1d6} plus your Sorcerer level in {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} (no action required).

entries.name: Innate Bladework
entries: Whenever you attack with a weapon with which you have proficiency while your {@classFeature Innate Sorcery|Sorcerer|XPHB|1} feature is active, you can use your Charisma modifier for the attack and damage rolls instead of using Strength or Dexterity.

entries.name: Martial Training
entries: You gain proficiency with Martial weapons and training with Light armor, Medium armor, and {@item Shield|XPHB|Shields}.

subclassFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn. Moreover, you can cast one of your Sorcerer cantrips that has a casting time of an action in place of one of those attacks.

subclassFeature.name: Mystical Maneuvers
entries: You can use the following martial maneuvers.

entries.name: Blinding Attack
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using a weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can expend two Sorcery Points as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to attempt to blind the target. Add {@damage 2d8} to the attack's damage roll. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC or have the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. At the end of each of its turns, the target repeats the save, ending the condition on itself on a success.

entries.name: Ruinous Blow
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using a weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can expend two Sorcery Points as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to sunder its defenses. Add {@damage 2d8} to the attack's damage roll. The target has a -3 penalty to AC until the end of your next turn.

entries.name: Wounding Strike
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using a weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can expend two Sorcery Points as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to deal the target a bleeding wound. Add {@dice 2d8} to the attack's damage roll. A creature with a bleeding wound can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and takes Necrotic damage equal to {@dice 1d8} for each of its wounds at the start of each of its turns. This damage ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB}. A creature can take an action to staunch all of its bleeding wounds.

subclassFeature.name: Heroic Haste
entries: Whenever you start casting {@spell Haste|XPHB} targeting yourself, you can modify it so that it doesn't require {@status Concentration|XPHB}. If you do so, when the spell ends, you don't have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition or a {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} of 0.

spell.name: Arc Blade

m.text: a Melee weapon with which you have proficiency and that is worth 1+ CP

entries: As lightning surges through the weapon used in the spell's casting, you make one attack. This attack can be a melee attack or ranged spell attack using the weapon with a range of 15 feet, as a wave of energy extends from the weapon. The attack uses your spellcasting ability for the attack and damage rolls instead of using Strength or Dexterity. If it is a ranged attack, it deals Lightning damage. Otherwise, it can deal Lightning damage or the weapon's normal damage type (your choice).

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: Whether you deal Lightning damage or the weapon's normal damage type, the attack deals extra Lightning damage when you reach levels 5 ({@dice 1d6}), 11 ({@dice 2d6}), and 17 ({@dice 3d6}).

spell.name: Burning Blade

m.text: a Melee weapon with which you have proficiency and that is worth 1+ CP

entries: Encasing the weapon used in the spell's casting in flames, you make one melee attack. The attack uses your spellcasting ability for the attack and damage rolls instead of using Strength or Dexterity. If the attack deals damage, it can be Fire damage or the weapon's normal damage type (your choice). If the weapon's damage die or any of the spell's damage dice roll the highest number, you can roll that die again and add it to the damage, rolling again if it is the highest number, and so on. You can add a maximum number of dice to the damage roll equal to your spellcasting ability modifier.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: Whether you deal Fire damage or the weapon's normal damage type, the attack deals extra Fire damage when you reach levels 5 ({@dice 1d6}), 11 ({@dice 2d6}), and 17 ({@dice 3d6}).

spell.name: Frigid Blade

m.text: a Melee weapon with which you have proficiency and that is worth 1+ CP

entries: Encasing the weapon used in the spell's casting in ice, you make one melee attack. The attack uses your spellcasting ability for the attack and damage rolls instead of using Strength or Dexterity. The weapon's damage die increases by 1 step ({@dice d4} → {@dice d6} → {@dice d8} → {@dice d10} → {@dice d12} or {@dice 2d6}, your choice). The weapon's damage dice don't increase if it has more than one weapon damage die. If the attack deals damage, it can be Cold damage or the weapon's normal damage type (your choice).

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: Whether you deal Cold damage or the weapon's normal damage type, the attack deals extra Cold damage when you reach levels 5 ({@dice 1d6}), 11 ({@dice 2d6}), and 17 ({@dice 3d6}).

spell.name: Candy Blast
entries: You summon a handful of hard candy—boiled sweets, mints, jelly beans, etc.—and hurl them at a creature you can see within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d8} Force damage and the candy forms a pile on the ground in its space, causing a 5-foot-square area of your choice in the target's space to become {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}. The candy produced by this spell is edible but has no nutritional value, and vanishes after 1 minute, ending any {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} when you reach levels 5 ({@dice 2d8}), 11 ({@dice 3d8}), and 17 ({@dice 4d8}).

spell.name: Cheat
entries: You twist your fingers and fate seems to follow suit. Until the end of your next turn, you can reroll any ability check you make to play nonmagical games of skill and must use the new roll. Therefore, this spell could influence a game of poker, but not the result of a {@item Deck of Many Things|XDMG}.

spell.name: Flawed Reconstruction
entries: You stitch together the wounds of a creature you touch, which regains {@dice 3d6} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. However, the mending is imperfect, and the target's {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum is reduced by {@dice 1d6}, to a minimum of 1.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The healing increases by {@dice 2d6} and the reduction increases by {@dice 1d6} for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Transient Bulwark

m.text: a pearl worth 10+ GP, which the spell consumes

entries: A fragile, invisible shield protects you for the duration. The next attack roll against you has a -10 penalty to hit, and the spell ends.

spell.name: Zephyr's Feather
entries: You wave your hand and four alabaster feathers, magically sharpened to a razor's edge, appear and orbit around you until the spell ends. When you cast this spell and as {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on your later turns, you can expend one of the feathers and make a ranged spell attack using it against a creature you can see within 120 feet of yourself. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d8} Force damage.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Blunder

time.condition: which you take when a creature you can see within range hits a target with an attack roll

entries: The attacker must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or suffer one of the following effects of your choice:

entries.name: Fumble
entries: The target drops one object of your choice that it's holding, with the object landing in a space you choose up to 10 feet away from the target. If the target drops the weapon it is using for the attack roll, it can make an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} for the triggering attack.

entries.name: Prat Fall
entries: The target has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. The {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} from the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition applies against the triggering attack, potentially causing it to miss.

spell.name: Protect Threshold

components.m: an ounce of salt for each foot of the warded portal's perimeter

entries: Tracing arcane sigils along its boundary, you can ward a doorway, window, or other portal from entry. For the duration, an {@condition Invisible|XPHB} eldritch creature stalks the warded portal. Any creature that attempts to pass through the portal makes a Wisdom saving throw, taking {@damage 4d6} Psychic damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful save.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for each spell slot level above 2.

spell.name: Stone Bones
entries: Choose a creature you can see within range. The target has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage until the spell ends at the end of your next turn.

spell.name: Swift Flight
entries: Streaks of energy flow from the back of a creature you touch, tracing the shapes of wings. The target has a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 60 feet and can hover until the end of its next turn. When the spell ends, the target falls if it is in the air and nothing is holding it aloft.

spell.name: Cosmic Horror
entries: For the briefest moment, you open a portal to an eldritch dimension in a 10-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on a point you can see within range. Each creature within the {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} makes a Wisdom saving throw as it beholds the nightmarish dimension. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 6d6} Psychic damage and has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for the duration. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage only.
A creature {@condition Frightened|XPHB} by this spell makes another Wisdom saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for each spell slot level above 3.

spell.name: Flashback
entries: Casting your mind through time, you make a subtle alteration to history, allowing you to make preparations for the current situation within the last 24 hours. Describe to your GM how the precautions you took ahead of time to affect the current situation. For example, you might have packed a particular piece of gear or arranged for a distraction to occur at this exact moment. The GM might call for an ability check if the preparations are sufficiently challenging; on a failure, the spell fails and has no effect.

A Flashback's effects are limited, as reality reasserts itself with the fewest possible changes to accommodate the alteration in history. The spell can't remove creatures or objects from an encounter, harm a creature, or cause creatures and objects that aren't being worn or carried by you to move. It can generally add an element to an encounter, but can't generally subtract them. The GM decides on the extent of the Flashback's effect.

spell.name: Rusting Grasp
entries: You conjure a potent acid at your fingertips, which can corrode metal and burn flesh. Make a melee spell attack against a creature or object within your reach. On a hit, a creature takes {@damage 6d4} Acid damage and it has a -3 penalty to its AC, to a minimum of 10, for 1 hour.

If you target a nonmagical metal object with this spell that isn't being worn or carried, you corrode and destroy parts of the object you choose that can fit within a 1-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube}.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 2d4} for each spell slot level above 3. If you target a nonmagical object, you can destroy an additional cubic foot for each slot level above 3.

spell.name: Séance

m.text: a crystal ball, deck of tarot cards, or ouija board, and incense worth 50+ GP

entries: You and three or more willing creatures lock hands to conjure a spirit from the afterlife to answer your questions. Describe or name a creature that is familiar to you. If the creature's soul is free and willing, it manifests as a ghostly specter. This spell fails if the spirit was the target of this spell within the last 10 days.

Until the spell ends, you can ask up to three questions of the specter. The specter knows only what it knew in life, including the languages it knew. Answers are usually brief, cryptic, or repetitive, and the specter is under no compulsion to offer a truthful answer if you are hostile to it or it recognizes you as an enemy. There is a {@chance 5} chance that this spell contacts the wrong spirit, one which will answer questions untruthfully or ambiguously.

spell.name: Mandy's Marvelous Dress
entries: A willing creature you touch is instantly clothed in a fabulous ball gown, perfectly tailored and complete with a set of glittering jewelry. You can choose for the dress to sit atop the creature's clothing or armor, or for it to magically replace its outfit. The creature can't remove the dress until the spell ends; similarly, the jewelry vanishes if it is removed.

entries.name: Elegant Flair
entries: Until the spell ends, when the wearer makes a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} using Charisma, it can replace the number it rolls with a 10.

entries.name: Glamorous Ward
entries: If the wearer of the dress is attacked by a creature that can see it, the attacker must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or miss with the attack. On a success, the attacker is immune to this effect until the spell ends.

entries.name: Duration
entries: This spell's duration varies depending upon when and where it was cast. If cast in a location where time passes normally, the spell lasts until the twelfth stroke of the next midnight, however long that may be. Elsewhere, the spell lasts for 24 hours. When the spell ends, the dress vanishes in a puff of sparkles, and the creature becomes clothed in its original outfit.

spell.name: Soul Effigy
entries: A Humanoid you touch makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a fragment of its soul is bound to the doll used in this spell's casting, which becomes an effigy of that creature. The spell ends if the effigy is destroyed or if the target is on another plane of existence. For the duration, you can use a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to do one of the following.

entries.name: Control
entries: You directly control the target's actions and movement on its next turn by manipulating the effigy's limbs. That target can only take the {@action Dash|XPHB}, {@action Disengage|XPHB}, {@action Hide|XPHB}, or {@action Utilize|XPHB} action. While controlled, the target's movements are jerky and unnatural.

entries.name: Harm
entries: You prod the effigy and deal {@damage 2d8} damage to the target. This damage is Bludgeoning, Piercing, Slashing, or another damage type, appropriate to the means by which you harm the effigy. This damage ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB}.

entries.name: Restrain
entries: You submerge the effigy in water, causing the target to believe it is suffocating. The target has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn.

spell.name: Sword of Judgment
entries: This spell designates an area of protection in a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on a point you choose within range. Within that {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere}, a spectral sword appears over each creature, following the creature as long as it remains within the {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere}.

Whenever a creature within the {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} makes an attack or casts a spell, you can force that creature to make a Dexterity saving throw, as the sword above it falls. Resolve the Dexterity saving throw before the triggering attack or spell. On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 4d8} Force damage, or half as much damage on a successful save. A creature makes this save only once per turn.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for each spell slot level above 5.

spell.name: Frenzy
entries: With a gesture, you reduce a crowd to base instinct and violence. Each creature in a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on a point you choose within range makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a creature has the Frenzied condition for the duration.

A creature with the Frenzied condition loses the ability to distinguish between friend and foe, regarding all creatures it can see as enemies. While Frenzied, the creature chooses the targets for its attacks, spells, and abilities randomly from among the creatures it can see within range, and it must make an {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB} if any creature provokes one.
At the end of its turn, an affected creature can repeat its saving throw, ending the condition on itself early on a success.

spell.name: Rocks Fall
entries: Boulders appear in the air and fall in a 60-foot-radius, 120-foot-high {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder} centered on a point within range. Each creature in the {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder} makes a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes {@damage 10d8} Bludgeoning damage and is buried under the rubble. On a successful save, a target takes half as much damage only.

A buried creature has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} and {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions, and can take an action to make a Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check against your spell save DC, freeing itself from the boulders and ending the {@condition Prone|XPHB} and {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions on itself on a success.
